message: Jalapeño corn dog bites. That's what we're doing this week.

The real move is the cornmeal crust absorbing the oil differently than batter would - it stays crisp longer, which matters for storage and reheating. That's the angle: technique as practicality, not just flavor.
You're right about the oil absorption - that's the actual story here, not some Instagram plating nonsense.
Maybe we lead with the storage angle instead - like, people actually reheat these things, right?

The thing that sells this isn't the crispness itself - it's that it *lasts*, which is why Margaret's method matters more than the initial bite. People need to know these don't turn to cardboard by day three.
So we're going with the longevity angle - that these actually *work* when people make them at home, not just look pretty on day one. That feels right to me. Good work today, everyone.
